relations: The Anglo-Saxons arrived in Britain and influenced the development of Old English
Old English evolved significantly after the Norman Conquest of England
The Norman Conquest of England established the Normans as the ruling class
The Normans' language, Old Norman, mixed with Old English to give rise to Middle English
Geoffrey Chaucer's works, such as 'The Canterbury Tales', are examples of Middle English literature
William Caxton's introduction of the printing press helped standardize Middle English
William Caxton's printing press contributed significantly to the spread and standardization of the English language
The Early Modern English period saw further development and changes in the English language
The British Empire's global influence was instrumental in spreading the English language worldwide
In the 20th century, English became the global lingua franca, further cementing its importance worldwide
William Shakespeare's works exemplify the language of the Early Modern English period
The Great Vowel Shift was a significant linguistic event during the Early Modern English period
